objects.content: Microsoft Cloud: Azure, Microsoft 365, Dynamics 365, Microsoft Teams, Microsoft Power Platform, LinkedIn, GitHub, Nuance.
AI platform Azure AI, OpenAI partnership, GitHub Copilot, Microsoft 365 Copilot, Bing Chat, Windows Copilot.
Intelligent Data Platform: Microsoft Fabric, databases, analytics, governance.
Hybrid infrastructure: Azure Arc, end-to-end security, compliance, identity, management.
Gaming: Xbox, Xbox Game Pass, Activision Blizzard acquisition.
Surface devices, Windows 11, Microsoft Edge browser.
Cloud economies of scale: lower cost per unit, improved utilization, lower maintenance labor costs.
Microsoft Cloud for Sustainability: environmental sustainability management platform.
Empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more.
Create local opportunity, growth, and impact in every country around the world.
Core principles: fairness, reliability and safety, privacy and security, inclusiveness, transparency, and accountability.
Commitment to being a carbon negative, water positive, and zero waste company by 2030.
Acquisition of Activision Blizzard, Inc. announced in January 2022.
Acquisition of Nuance Communications in 2022.
Long-term partnership with OpenAI, including exclusive cloud provider agreement and increased investments in supercomputing systems.
Launch of AI-powered Microsoft Edge browser and Bing search engine with Bing Chat in February 2023.
Launch of Microsoft 365 Copilot, GitHub Copilot, Windows Copilot.
Launch of Microsoft Fabric, unified analytics platform.
Racial Equity Initiative: $150 million investment to strengthen inclusion and double Black, African American, Hispanic, and Latinx leaders in U.S. by 2025.
Skills for Jobs initiative and AI Skills Initiative launched in June 2023.
Long-term debt: various notes issued under indenture agreements (e.g., 3.125% Notes due 2028, 2.625% Notes due 2033).
Operating lease commitments for datacenters and office space.
Uncertain tax positions related to transfer pricing issues unresolved with IRS.
Contractual commitments for cloud infrastructure and datacenter expansion.
Indemnification obligations under Officers' and Directors' Indemnification Trust Agreements.
Intense competition in technology markets.
Rapid technological change and disruption.
Data privacy and security breaches.
Regulatory and legal compliance risks (including antitrust, trade, tax).
Economic downturns and global economic uncertainty.
Geopolitical instability and trade restrictions.
Dependence on third-party suppliers and partners.
Intellectual property infringement claims.
Currency fluctuations and foreign exchange risk.
Ability to attract and retain talent.
Climate change and sustainability risks.
Reinvent productivity and business processes.
Build the intelligent cloud and intelligent edge platform.
Create more personal computing.
Invest in AI across the entire company, infusing generative AI into consumer and commercial offerings.
Transform the workplace with modern business applications, deeper insights, and improved collaboration.
Build and run cloud-based services utilizing ubiquitous computing.
Apply AI and ambient intelligence to drive insights and productivity gains.
Tackle security with integrated end-to-end solutions across all clouds and platforms.
Invent new gaming experiences that bring people together across devices.
Use Windows to fuel cloud business, grow PC market share, and drive engagement with services.
Invest in high performance and sustainable computing to meet growing demand for cloud services.
Expand cybersecurity workforce: skill and recruit 250,000 people by 2025.
Microsoft Cloud: Azure, Microsoft 365, Dynamics 365, LinkedIn, GitHub, and Nuance.
AI offerings including Copilot, Copilot stack, Azure AI, GitHub Copilot, and Copilot for Security.
Custom AI accelerator Azure Maia and custom central processing unit Azure Cobalt.
Hybrid infrastructure with Azure Arc for consistent multi-cloud and on-premises management.
Microsoft Intelligent Data Platform integrating databases, analytics, and governance.
Xbox Game Pass subscription service and Activision Blizzard game portfolio.
Windows 11, Surface devices, Copilot+ PCs, Microsoft Edge, and Bing search engine.
Long-term partnership with OpenAI, exclusive cloud provider for OpenAI workloads.
Empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more.
Committed to making digital technology and artificial intelligence available broadly and doing so responsibly.
Strive to create local opportunity, growth, and impact in every country around the world.
Committed to being a carbon negative, water positive, and zero waste company by 2030.
Acquisition of Activision Blizzard, Inc. completed in October 2023.
Increased investments in OpenAI and specialized supercomputing systems.
Launch of AI Skills initiative and expansion of cybersecurity workforce programs.
Commitment to sustainability goals: carbon negative, water positive, zero waste by 2030.
Long-term debt including notes with various maturities (e.g., 3.125% Notes due 2028, 2.625% Notes due 2033).
Operating lease commitments for facilities and datacenters.
Legal contingencies including tax disputes and regulatory matters.
Contractual commitments for cloud infrastructure and AI investments.
Intense competition in cloud computing, AI, and other technology markets.
Regulatory and legal challenges related to AI, data privacy, and antitrust.
Cybersecurity threats and data breaches.
Economic downturns affecting customer spending.
Rapid technological changes and the need to innovate.
Geopolitical tensions and trade restrictions impacting global operations.
Reinvent productivity and business processes.
Build the intelligent cloud and intelligent edge platform.
Create more personal computing.
Invest in high performance and sustainable computing to meet growing demand for cloud and AI infrastructure.
Lead the AI platform wave across solution areas.
Expand gaming experiences on PC, console, mobile, and cloud.
Use Windows to fuel cloud business, grow PC market share, and drive engagement with services.

The Microsoft Cloud provides integration across the technology stack while offering openness, improving time to value, reducing costs, and increasing agility. Our cloud business benefits from three economies of scale: datacenters that deploy computational resources at significantly lower cost per unit than smaller ones; datacenters that coordinate and aggregate diverse customer, geographic, and application demand patterns, improving the utilization of computing, storage, and network resources; and multi-tenancy locations that lower application maintenance labor costs.

Azure is a comprehensive set of cloud services that offer developers, IT professionals, and enterprises freedom to build, deploy, and manage applications on any platform or device. Customers can use Azure through our global network of datacenters for computing, networking, storage, mobile and web application services, AI, Internet of Things, cognitive services, and machine learning.

Azure AI offerings provide a competitive advantage as companies seek ways to optimize and scale their business with AI. We offer supercomputing power for AI at scale to run large workloads, complemented by our rapidly expanding portfolio of AI cloud services (including the latest models) and hardware, which includes custom-built silicon and strong partnerships with chip manufacturers. Azure AI Foundry is a unified platform for developers to design, customize, and manage AI applications and agents.

Microsoft 365 Commercial is an AI-powered business and productivity solutions platform that brings together Office, Windows, Microsoft 365 Copilot, and Enterprise Mobility + Security to help organizations empower their employees.

LinkedIn connects the world’s professionals to make them more productive and successful and transforms the way companies hire, market, sell, and learn. LinkedIn offers monetized solutions: Talent Solutions, Marketing Solutions, Premium Subscriptions, and Sales Solutions.

Dynamics provides cloud-based and on-premises business solutions for financial management, enterprise resource planning (ERP), customer relationship management (CRM), and supply chain management, as well as agentic AI and other low code application development platforms.
GitHub provides a collaboration platform for developers to manage code and incorporate AI and agent-based tools across the software development lifecycle.
Nuance Healthcare provides AI solutions to the healthcare industry.

We offer an array of services, including cloud-based solutions that provide customers with software, services, platforms, and content, and we provide solution support and consulting services. We also deliver relevant online advertising to a global audience.

Our products include operating systems, cross-device productivity and collaboration applications, server applications, business solution applications, desktop and server management tools, software development tools, and video games. We also design and sell devices, including PCs, tablets, gaming and entertainment consoles, other intelligent devices, and related accessories.
We offer our customers integrated AI-driven products addressing security, compliance, identity, management, and privacy across customers’ multi-cloud, application, and device assets.

Azure’s competitive advantage includes enabling a hybrid cloud, allowing deployment of existing datacenters with our public cloud into a single, cohesive infrastructure, and the ability to run at a scale that meets the needs of businesses of all sizes and complexities.

We believe our cloud’s global scale, coupled with our broad portfolio of identity and security solutions, allows us to effectively solve complex cybersecurity challenges for our customers and differentiates us from the competition.
Our mission is to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more.
We are committed to designing and deploying AI responsibly with safety and security from the outset.
We prioritize security above all else.

Microsoft is committed to sustainability and our approach to addressing climate change starts with the sustainability of our own business. In 2020, we announced goals to become a carbon negative, water positive, and zero waste company by 2030.
In August 2024, we announced changes to the composition of our segments, bringing the commercial components of Microsoft 365 together in the Productivity and Business Processes segment.
We completed the acquisition of Activision Blizzard in fiscal year 2024, expanding our gaming content and intellectual property.
We continue to invest in gaming studios and content to expand our intellectual property roadmap and leverage new content creators.
We have strong partnerships with chip manufacturers for AI hardware.

We have outstanding notes, including 3.125% Notes due 2028, 2.625% Notes due 2033, and various other senior notes issued under indentures with The Bank of New York Mellon Trust Company, N.A. and U.S. Bank National Association.

We have assumed Activision Blizzard's senior notes, including 3.400% Senior Notes due 2026, 3.400% Senior Notes due 2027, 1.350% Senior Notes due 2030, 4.500% Senior Notes due 2047, and 2.500% Senior Notes due 2050.
We have operating and finance lease obligations for datacenters, office space, and other facilities.
We are subject to ongoing IRS audits for tax years subsequent to 2003, with unresolved transfer pricing issues that could result in material tax liabilities.
We have contractual commitments to purchase goods and services, including cloud infrastructure and other operational needs.

We face intense competition in all our markets, including from cloud service providers, open source offerings, AI products from hyperscalers and emerging competitors, and various software and device vendors.
Our business depends on our ability to attract and retain talented employees. Competition for qualified personnel is intense.
Cyberattacks, security vulnerabilities, and data breaches could harm our reputation, disrupt our operations, and result in significant liability.
We are subject to complex and evolving laws and regulations regarding data privacy, security, AI, and other areas, which could increase our costs and limit our business.
Our global operations expose us to geopolitical risks, including trade restrictions, sanctions, and political instability.
We may not realize the expected benefits of our acquisitions, including the acquisition of Activision Blizzard, due to integration challenges or other factors.
Our business is subject to economic cycles and downturns, which could reduce demand for our products and services.
We face risks related to the development and deployment of AI, including ethical concerns, regulatory scrutiny, and potential misuse.
Our intellectual property portfolio may not prevent competitors from developing similar products, and we may face claims of infringement.
We have significant debt and lease obligations, and our credit ratings could be downgraded, increasing our borrowing costs.
We are subject to ongoing tax audits and disputes, including with the IRS regarding transfer pricing, which could result in material tax liabilities.
Our cloud services depend on the availability and reliability of our datacenters and network infrastructure; any disruption could harm our business.
Changes in foreign currency exchange rates could adversely affect our financial results.
Reinvent productivity and business processes to help organizations and individuals work and collaborate more securely and efficiently.

Build the intelligent cloud and intelligent edge platform to provide a foundation for our customers’ digital workloads including hybrid consistency, developer productivity, data and AI capabilities, and trusted security and compliance.
Create more personal computing to enable users to interact with technology in more intuitive, engaging, and dynamic ways.
We are focused on helping customers use the breadth and depth of the Microsoft Cloud to get the most value out of their digital spend while leading the AI platform wave across our solution areas.

We are investing significant resources in: Transforming the workplace to deliver new, modern, modular business applications, drive deeper insights, and improve how people communicate, collaborate, learn, work, and interact with one another.

We are investing significant resources in: Building and running cloud-based services in ways that utilize ubiquitous computing to unleash new experiences and opportunities for businesses and individuals.

We are investing significant resources in: Applying AI and ambient intelligence to drive insights, revolutionize many types of work and business processes, and provide substantive productivity gains using Microsoft 365 Copilot and agents.

We are investing significant resources in: Providing training on generative AI and greater access to digital learning and resources through skilling programs and initiatives, grants, and LinkedIn learning pathways.

We are investing significant resources in: Inventing new gaming experiences that bring people together around their shared love for games on any device and pushing the boundaries of innovation with console and PC gaming.

We are investing significant resources in: Leveraging Windows to fuel our cloud business, grow our share of the PC market, and drive increased engagement with our services like Microsoft Edge, Bing, Copilot, Microsoft Teams, Microsoft 365 Consumer, Xbox Game Pass, and more.

We are investing significant resources in: Tackling security from all angles with our integrated, end-to-end solutions spanning security, compliance, identity, and management, across all clouds and platforms.
Our goal is to lead the industry in several distinct areas of technology over the long term, which we expect will translate to sustained growth.
Our future growth depends on our ability to transcend current product category definitions, business models, and sales motions.

Xbox Game Pass subscription service and Activision Blizzard game portfolio.
Windows 11, Surface devices, Copilot+ PCs, Microsoft Edge, and Bing search engine.
Long-term partnership with OpenAI, exclusive cloud provider for OpenAI workloads.

Committed to being a carbon negative, water positive, and zero waste company by 2030.

changes.content_after: Our mission is to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more.
We are committed to designing and deploying AI responsibly with safety and security from the outset.
We prioritize security above all else.

Microsoft is committed to sustainability and our approach to addressing climate change starts with the sustainability of our own business. In 2020, we announced goals to become a carbon negative, water positive, and zero waste company by 2030.

changes.content_before: Acquisition of Activision Blizzard, Inc. completed in October 2023.
Increased investments in OpenAI and specialized supercomputing systems.
Launch of AI Skills initiative and expansion of cybersecurity workforce programs.
Commitment to sustainability goals: carbon negative, water positive, zero waste by 2030.

changes.content_after: In August 2024, we announced changes to the composition of our segments, bringing the commercial components of Microsoft 365 together in the Productivity and Business Processes segment.
We completed the acquisition of Activision Blizzard in fiscal year 2024, expanding our gaming content and intellectual property.
We continue to invest in gaming studios and content to expand our intellectual property roadmap and leverage new content creators.
We have strong partnerships with chip manufacturers for AI hardware.

changes.content_before: Long-term debt including notes with various maturities (e.g., 3.125% Notes due 2028, 2.625% Notes due 2033).
Operating lease commitments for facilities and datacenters.
Legal contingencies including tax disputes and regulatory matters.
Contractual commitments for cloud infrastructure and AI investments.

changes.content_after: We have outstanding notes, including 3.125% Notes due 2028, 2.625% Notes due 2033, and various other senior notes issued under indentures with The Bank of New York Mellon Trust Company, N.A. and U.S. Bank National Association.

We have assumed Activision Blizzard's senior notes, including 3.400% Senior Notes due 2026, 3.400% Senior Notes due 2027, 1.350% Senior Notes due 2030, 4.500% Senior Notes due 2047, and 2.500% Senior Notes due 2050.
We have operating and finance lease obligations for datacenters, office space, and other facilities.
We are subject to ongoing IRS audits for tax years subsequent to 2003, with unresolved transfer pricing issues that could result in material tax liabilities.
We have contractual commitments to purchase goods and services, including cloud infrastructure and other operational needs.

changes.content_before: Intense competition in cloud computing, AI, and other technology markets.
Regulatory and legal challenges related to AI, data privacy, and antitrust.
Cybersecurity threats and data breaches.
Economic downturns affecting customer spending.
Rapid technological changes and the need to innovate.
Geopolitical tensions and trade restrictions impacting global operations.

changes.content_after: We face intense competition in all our markets, including from cloud service providers, open source offerings, AI products from hyperscalers and emerging competitors, and various software and device vendors.
Our business depends on our ability to attract and retain talented employees. Competition for qualified personnel is intense.
Cyberattacks, security vulnerabilities, and data breaches could harm our reputation, disrupt our operations, and result in significant liability.
We are subject to complex and evolving laws and regulations regarding data privacy, security, AI, and other areas, which could increase our costs and limit our business.
Our global operations expose us to geopolitical risks, including trade restrictions, sanctions, and political instability.
We may not realize the expected benefits of our acquisitions, including the acquisition of Activision Blizzard, due to integration challenges or other factors.
Our business is subject to economic cycles and downturns, which could reduce demand for our products and services.
We face risks related to the development and deployment of AI, including ethical concerns, regulatory scrutiny, and potential misuse.
Our intellectual property portfolio may not prevent competitors from developing similar products, and we may face claims of infringement.
We have significant debt and lease obligations, and our credit ratings could be downgraded, increasing our borrowing costs.
We are subject to ongoing tax audits and disputes, including with the IRS regarding transfer pricing, which could result in material tax liabilities.
Our cloud services depend on the availability and reliability of our datacenters and network infrastructure; any disruption could harm our business.
Changes in foreign currency exchange rates could adversely affect our financial results.

Cybersecurity threats and data breaches.

Geopolitical tensions and trade restrictions impacting global operations.

changes.content_before: Reinvent productivity and business processes.
Build the intelligent cloud and intelligent edge platform.
Create more personal computing.
Invest in high performance and sustainable computing to meet growing demand for cloud and AI infrastructure.
Lead the AI platform wave across solution areas.
Expand gaming experiences on PC, console, mobile, and cloud.
Use Windows to fuel cloud business, grow PC market share, and drive engagement with services.

changes.content_after: Reinvent productivity and business processes to help organizations and individuals work and collaborate more securely and efficiently.

Build the intelligent cloud and intelligent edge platform to provide a foundation for our customers’ digital workloads including hybrid consistency, developer productivity, data and AI capabilities, and trusted security and compliance.
Create more personal computing to enable users to interact with technology in more intuitive, engaging, and dynamic ways.
We are focused on helping customers use the breadth and depth of the Microsoft Cloud to get the most value out of their digital spend while leading the AI platform wave across our solution areas.

We are investing significant resources in: Transforming the workplace to deliver new, modern, modular business applications, drive deeper insights, and improve how people communicate, collaborate, learn, work, and interact with one another.

We are investing significant resources in: Building and running cloud-based services in ways that utilize ubiquitous computing to unleash new experiences and opportunities for businesses and individuals.

We are investing significant resources in: Applying AI and ambient intelligence to drive insights, revolutionize many types of work and business processes, and provide substantive productivity gains using Microsoft 365 Copilot and agents.

We are investing significant resources in: Providing training on generative AI and greater access to digital learning and resources through skilling programs and initiatives, grants, and LinkedIn learning pathways.

We are investing significant resources in: Inventing new gaming experiences that bring people together around their shared love for games on any device and pushing the boundaries of innovation with console and PC gaming.

We are investing significant resources in: Leveraging Windows to fuel our cloud business, grow our share of the PC market, and drive increased engagement with our services like Microsoft Edge, Bing, Copilot, Microsoft Teams, Microsoft 365 Consumer, Xbox Game Pass, and more.

We are investing significant resources in: Tackling security from all angles with our integrated, end-to-end solutions spanning security, compliance, identity, and management, across all clouds and platforms.
Our goal is to lead the industry in several distinct areas of technology over the long term, which we expect will translate to sustained growth.
Our future growth depends on our ability to transcend current product category definitions, business models, and sales motions.
